news.title: Satellite devices bought to settle property disputes in Gurugram have a ‘crash’-landing
news.para: Gurugram's multi-crore satellite land survey machines are failing to deliver, showing errors of up to 20 feet.  These glitches, coupled with overheating tablets and untrained operators, are creating new disputes instead of resolving old ones.  Officials are now calling for urgent hardware-software reviews and better training to salvage the project.

news.title: Gurugram civic body nets ₹213 crore property tax in first quarter, crosses 65% of annual target
news.para: Gurugram's Municipal Corporation has collected a significant Rs 213 crore in property tax during the first quarter of FY 2026-27, achieving 65.5% of its annual target. This surge, particularly in June, was driven by a state government rebate and interest waiver scheme, with the collected revenue earmarked for crucial civic amenities like roads and sanitation.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh fixes deadline for takeover of township by civic bodies
news.para: Uttar Pradesh government has introduced strict timelines for private townships to be handed over to municipal corporations, aiming to resolve long-standing maintenance disputes. This new procedure mandates joint inspections and financial deposits for incomplete civic infrastructure, potentially easing the double burden of taxes and private charges faced by thousands of residents. While future handovers may accelerate, completion certificate delays could still leave some townships in administrative limbo.

news.title: Kolkata: Audit panel to review 60 high-rises in New Town
news.para: Authorities are scrutinizing around 60 high-rise residential and commercial buildings in New Town constructed over the past three years. A high-level audit committee has begun verifying these structures, prioritizing under-construction institutional, educational, and IT buildings. This move follows complaints of residential properties being misused for commercial purposes, with many buildings suspected of violating land-use norms.

news.title: Sale of 100 plots in Hyderabad’s Mokila fetches HMDA ₹231 crore
news.para: Hyderabad Metropolitan Development Authority has achieved a record Rs 1.09 lakh per sq yard bid at its Mokila layout auction, more than double the upset price. This e-auction, spanning two days, saw 100 residential plots fetch Rs 231 crore, highlighting strong market confidence and demand for well-planned communities. Similar success was noted in Medipally, reinforcing HMDA's effective land monetization strategy.

news.title: Mumbai: Century-old Edwardian bungalow on Pali Hill to make way for tower
news.para: Bandra's historic Franklin House, a 1920s bungalow, is slated for demolition. Aspect Realty, led by former BJP leader Mohit Kamboj, has acquired the prime Pali Hill property after a decades-long ownership dispute.  Market sources estimate the land's value at Rs 170 crore. Kamboj plans to construct a luxury residential tower on the site, having reportedly settled all outstanding issues and compensated tenants.

news.title: Godrej Properties acquires 4.95-acre land parcel in Noida for ₹331.75 crore
news.para: Godrej Properties has emerged as the highest bidder for a 4.95-acre residential land parcel in Sector 151, Noida, for ₹331.75 crore. The proposed residential project is expected to have revenue potential of over ₹2,000 crore.

news.title: Delhi civic body extends property tax rebate deadline till July 31
news.para: Property owners in Delhi now have until July 31 to claim a 10% rebate on their property tax for the 2026-27 financial year. The Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) extended the deadline to assist more taxpayers, especially those who missed the original June 30 cut-off. This move aims to ease payment for citizens and boost the MCD's revenue for essential civic services and development projects across the capital.

news.title: UK housebuilders face potential lawsuit over alleged anti-competitive conduct
news.para: Britain's top home builders, including Barratt and Taylor Wimpey, face a massive class-action lawsuit for alleged price-fixing. Over 700,000 homeowners could receive up to £4.5 billion in compensation. The claim alleges housebuilders shared sensitive pricing and sales data, inflating new-build costs. This follows a previous probe by the competition watchdog, which saw seven firms pay £100 million to settle concerns.

news.title: Institutional investments in Indian real estate rise 37% to $1.7 billion in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: Institutional investments in Indian real estate rose 37% year-on-year to $1.7 billion in Q1 2026, led by acquisitions of income-generating assets, according to Equirus Capital. Delhi-NCR remained a key investment market, supported by premium housing, Grade-A office demand and infrastructure-led growth corridors.

news.title: Install rainwater harvesting system, get 10% property tax rebate: Indore mayor
news.para: Indore is offering a 10% property tax rebate for buildings with functional rainwater harvesting systems to encourage groundwater conservation.  Mayor Pushyamitra Bhargav also reviewed urban transit plans, including the unveiling of a Swami Vivekananda statue and the naming of a new route as 'Atal Path' featuring an Atal Bihari Vajpayee statue. Discussions also covered road expansions and renaming proposals.

news.title: Lucknow development body to assign unique property IDs, create digital dossiers to curb fraud
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority is launching a digital property system, assigning unique IDs to all properties. This move aims to consolidate records like building maps, payment history, and legal status onto a single platform.  The initiative promises to significantly reduce paperwork, speed up verification, and combat fraud and ownership disputes, empowering buyers with clear legal status information before purchases.

news.title: Mumbai property registrations rise 6% to 80,221 units in H1 2026: Knight Frank India
news.para: Mumbai recorded 80,221 property registrations in H1 2026, the strongest first-half performance since 2013, according to Knight Frank India. Stamp duty collections rose 4% year-on-year to ₹6,968 crore during the period.

news.title: Raipur: 77 houses razed in anti-encroachment drive near airport
news.para: Authorities demolished 77 homes on government land near Raipur airport, sparking protests. Residents, including women, claimed many houses were built under government housing schemes like PMAY and that the land belonged to their ancestors. The administration stated notices were served and rehabilitation is being provided, while villagers allege the land was for common grazing and is now being cleared for a legislators' colony.

news.title: Cement volumes may grow 6-7% in FY27: ICRA
news.para: Cement volume growth is expected to moderate to 6-7% in FY27 after rising 8.6% in FY26, according to ICRA. Operating margins are likely to moderate by 150-250 basis points in FY27 due to higher input costs.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body freezes 81 properties over unpaid tax dues
news.para: Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation is aggressively pursuing property tax defaulters in the East Zone, aiming to recover over Rs 1.1 crore.  The civic body has placed encumbrances on 81 properties and initiated auction proceedings against 22 habitual defaulters.  Further stringent actions, including property sealing and utility disconnections, are planned for those who fail to clear their dues.

news.title: NCR housing launches fall 40% in Q2 2026, sales down 6%: Anarock
news.para: NCR recorded 11,205 housing launches in Q2 2026, down 40% year-on-year, while sales declined 6% to 13,365 units. Available inventory remained almost unchanged at 89,086 units at the end of the quarter.

news.title: Chandigarh housing board asks allottees to verify dues online
news.para: Chandigarh Housing Board is urging property holders to verify their outstanding dues online. Allottees and transferees under various schemes are advised to check their account statements on the CHB portal. Any discrepancies should be reported with original payment receipts at the CHB office. A public camp will also be held for reconciliation. This is a crucial opportunity to rectify records before demand notices are issued.

news.title: Musi oustees to get 2BHK houses: Telangana housing minister
news.para: Telangana's Revenue Minister Ponguleti Srinivas Reddy has assured families displaced by the Musi rejuvenation project will receive 2 BHK homes and lawful compensation. The ambitious project aims to transform the Musi River into a tourism and economic hub for Hyderabad, ensuring flood control, water treatment, and enhanced green spaces.  The government emphasized a humane approach, prioritizing the welfare of the poor and preventing any injustice in the name of development.
